name: Bitbucket
shortDescription: FlowRunner integration for [Bitbucket Cloud](https://bitbucket.org) via the Bitbucket Cloud REST API 2.0. Manage repositories, issues, pull requests, source files, branches, and pipelines within a workspace. Authenticates with HTTP Basic using your Atlassian account email plus an API token (App Passwords are deprecated).

actions.name: List Repositories
actions.description: Lists repositories in the configured workspace. Supports filtering by the caller's permission role, a BBQL query string, and sorting. Aggregates results across paginated responses.

actions.name: Get Repository
actions.description: Retrieves detailed information about a single repository in the configured workspace, including its main branch, size, language, and links.

actions.name: Create Issue
actions.description: Creates an issue in a repository's issue tracker. The issue tracker must be enabled for the repository. Kind and priority are optional and default to Bug and Major respectively.

actions.name: Get Issue
actions.description: Retrieves a single issue from a repository's issue tracker by its numeric ID.

actions.name: List Issues
actions.description: Lists issues in a repository's issue tracker. Supports an optional Bitbucket query language filter (e.g. state = "new"). Aggregates results across paginated responses.

actions.name: Update Issue
actions.description: Updates an existing issue. Only the fields you provide are changed. State transitions the issue (e.g. New, Resolved, Closed).

actions.name: Add Issue Comment
actions.description: Adds a comment to an existing issue in a repository's issue tracker.

actions.name: Create Pull Request
actions.description: Creates a pull request from a source branch into a destination branch. Optionally closes the source branch on merge and adds reviewers by account ID (comma-separated).

actions.name: Get Pull Request
actions.description: Retrieves a single pull request by its numeric ID, including source and destination branches, author, state, and links.

actions.name: List Pull Requests
actions.description: Lists pull requests in a repository, optionally filtered by state. Aggregates results across paginated responses.

actions.name: Update Pull Request
actions.description: Updates an existing pull request. Only the fields you provide are changed.

actions.name: Approve Pull Request
actions.description: Approves a pull request on behalf of the authenticated user.

actions.name: Unapprove Pull Request
actions.description: Removes the authenticated user's approval from a pull request.

actions.name: Merge Pull Request
actions.description: Merges a pull request into its destination branch using the selected merge strategy. Optionally sets a commit message and closes the source branch.

actions.name: Decline Pull Request
actions.description: Declines (rejects) an open pull request without merging it.

actions.name: Add Pull Request Comment
actions.description: Adds a comment to a pull request. Comments are rendered as Markdown.

actions.name: Get File
actions.description: Retrieves the raw contents of a file at a given commit or branch. The commit reference can be a branch name (e.g. main), a tag, or a full commit hash.

actions.name: List Directory
actions.description: Lists the contents of a directory in a repository at a given commit or branch. Returns files and subdirectories. Aggregates results across paginated responses.

actions.name: Create or Update File
actions.description: Creates or updates a file in a repository and commits the change to a branch. If the file exists it is overwritten; otherwise it is created. The commit is pushed to the specified branch.

actions.name: List Commits
actions.description: Lists commits in a repository, optionally starting from a specific branch, tag, or commit. Aggregates results across paginated responses.

actions.name: List Branches
actions.description: Lists branches in a repository. Aggregates results across paginated responses.

actions.name: Create Branch
actions.description: Creates a new branch pointing at a target commit hash. The target must be an existing commit in the repository.

actions.name: Delete Branch
actions.description: Deletes a branch from a repository. This action is permanent.

actions.name: List Pipelines
actions.description: Lists pipeline runs for a repository. Pipelines must be enabled for the repository. Aggregates results across paginated responses.

actions.name: Trigger Pipeline
actions.description: Triggers a new pipeline run for a branch. Pipelines must be enabled and a bitbucket-pipelines.yml must be present on the branch.

actions.name: Get Pipeline
actions.description: Retrieves a single pipeline run by its UUID, including its state and result.

actions.name: Stop Pipeline
actions.description: Stops a running pipeline by its UUID. Stopping is asynchronous; the pipeline moves to a stopped state shortly after.

idealUseCases: Automatically open, comment on, and update issues in response to events from other systems.
Create, review, approve, merge, or decline pull requests as part of a release workflow.
Commit generated or synced files to a branch and track the resulting commits.
Trigger, monitor, and stop CI/CD pipelines and report their status downstream.
Manage branches and browse source files and directories programmatically.
agentIdeas: When a **GitHub** "On Pull Request Opened" trigger fires, use **Bitbucket** "Create Pull Request" to mirror the change into a Bitbucket repository, then **Slack** "Send Message To Channel" to notify reviewers with the PR link.
Use **Jira** "Search Issues" to find issues marked ready for release, then call **Bitbucket** "Merge Pull Request" for each linked PR and **Bitbucket** "Trigger Pipeline" to deploy the merged branch.
After **Bitbucket** "List Pipelines" reports a failed run, use **Jira** "Create Issue" to file a bug and **Slack** "Send Message To Channel" to alert the on-call engineer with the pipeline details.
